LAW AND MOTION, 302, MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT BASED ON DEFAULT OF STIPULATED SETTLMENT IS DENIED. THE PORTION OF THE STIPULATION FOR SETTLEMENT CALLING FOR JUDGMENT TO BE ENTERED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250,000 IF ANY OR ALL OF PAYMENTS TOTALING $57,000 ARE NOT PAID IS AN UNENFORCEABLE PENALTY. MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF SEEKING A SUM THAT DOES NOT VIOLATE THE LIQUIDATION DAMAGES RULE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Vacated

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-12-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice And Motion To Enter Judgment Based On Default Of Stipulated Settlment
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. AARON R. JACKSON of Jackson Law Firm, Tel No. (415) 517-7533 specially appearing for attorney James Mark Millar with Plaintiff CARLOS BARRIOS;
Defendant Hugo Lemus, Tel No. (510) 432-6210 present in pro per.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CARLOS BARRIOS' Motion To Enter Judgment Based On Default Of Stipulated Settlment is denied. The portion of the stipulation for settlement calling for judgment to be entered in the amount of $250,000 if any or all of payments totaling $57,000 are not paid is an unenforceable penalty. (Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal. App. 4th 495; see also Jade Fashion & Co., Inc. v. Harkham Industries, Inc. (2014) 229 Cal. App. 4th 635, 645-651 (explaining the rule in the Greentree case)). Motion is denied without prejudice to Plaintiff seeking a sum that does not violate the liquidation damages rule. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)
